evidence: Double yellow center lines and white edge lines are standard road markings in Brazil.
Flat, arid plains with red soil and sparse tropical palms are characteristic of the Brazilian Cerrado and the transition to the Amazon/Caatinga (e.g., Mato Grosso, Tocantins, Maranhão).
The camera vehicle (white hood, black roof rails, black antenna) matches the Gen 4 Google Street View car frequently found in Brazil.
Hazy or smoky skies are common in the Brazilian interior during the dry season due to agricultural burning (queimadas).
contradictions: Double yellow lines are also used in North American countries like Mexico, but the specific landscape and vehicle meta strongly favor South America.
Red soil and palms can be found in Eastern Bolivia (Santa Cruz), though infrastructure and camera meta are more distinctive for Brazil.
